subject: History
chapter: Tribals, Dikus and the Vision of a Golden Age

activities_projects.title: Tribal Society Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the social structure of tribal communities
steps: Read about the lifestyle of tribal communities
List important features of tribal society
Explain any two features in 4–5 lines
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the organization of tribal communities
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and accuracy of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Concept Map – Tribals and Dikus
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between tribals and outsiders
steps: Write 'Tribals and Dikus' in the center
Add branches showing who the dikus were (moneylenders, traders, landlords)
Explain how they affected tribal life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students visualize relationships between social groups

activities_projects.title: Birsa Munda Research Notes
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the role of Birsa Munda in tribal resistance
steps: Read about Birsa Munda’s life and leadership
Write five important points about his movement
Explain why tribals followed him
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the significance of tribal leaders

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Chart – Tribal Revolt
steps: Write causes such as loss of land, forest laws, and exploitation
Draw arrows showing their effects
Explain how these causes led to rebellion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze historical causes and consequences
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical connection between causes and effects (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparison Table – Tribal Life Before and After British Rule
steps: Create two columns: Before British rule and After British rule
Write differences in land use, forest access, and livelihood
Explain one major change in detail
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the impact of colonial policies
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and depth of comparison (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Map Activity – Tribal Regions
activities_projects.objective: Locate areas where tribal communities lived
steps: Locate Chotanagpur and nearby tribal areas
Mark regions associated with Birsa Munda
Label them clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves geographical understanding of tribal movements

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Tribal Leader
activities_projects.objective: Understand the viewpoints of tribal communities
steps: Choose roles such as Birsa Munda, tribal farmers, and British officials
Act out a short scene showing a tribal protest
Discuss the issues faced by tribals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand historical perspectives through interaction

activities_projects.title: Flowchart – Birsa Movement
activities_projects.objective: Understand the sequence of events in Birsa’s movement
steps: List important events related to Birsa Munda
Arrange them in chronological order
Create a flowchart showing the development of the movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the timeline of tribal resistance

activities_projects.title: Short Essay – Vision of a Golden Age
activities_projects.objective: Understand Birsa Munda’s ideas and goals
steps: Write a short essay explaining Birsa’s vision of a Golden Age
Mention what changes he wanted for tribal society
Explain why people believed in his ideas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret historical ideas and beliefs
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster – Tribal Resistance
steps: Create a poster showing tribal resistance against exploitation
Include symbols or drawings related to forests and tribal life
Explain your poster to the class
